subject: Geography
title: Google Earth's Revolution
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Google Earth's Revolution.

hook.text: In 2005, Google Earth gave everyone a satellite view of the entire planet for free.

buildup.text: For the first time, anyone could zoom from space to their own rooftop in seconds.

discovery.text: Archaeologists used it to find ancient ruins. Activists tracked deforestation.

twist.text: Some governments demanded areas be blurred—military bases, nuclear sites, even entire countries.

climax.text: Free satellite imagery democratized mapping and made secrecy nearly impossible.

punchline.text: When everyone can see everything, hiding gets harder.

quiz.question: What year did Google Earth launch?
